etymology.description: Depicts a bed -> original form of 牀 (chuáng, "bed") -> contracted form in 臧 (zāng, "possess")
etymology.interpretation: The character 爿 (pán, "bed") visually represents a bed, emphasizing its usage in denoting places for resting or sleeping. It served as the original form for the character 牀 (chuáng, "bed"), reinforcing its meaning. Additionally, in the character 臧 (zāng, "possess"), it appears as a contracted form, reflecting its historical usage in composite forms.
